rag_rules: Fix AGREE diagnostics by resolving undefined identifiers or invalid expressions before adding new clauses.
Avoid introducing package-qualified names or types not present in the AADL model.
Do not add new architecture elements when repairing AGREE contract issues.
generic_rules: When AGREE reports type errors, ensure all identifiers and helper declarations are valid and visible in the current scope.
Use only declared constants, variables, or features that exist in the AADL model when writing AGREE contracts.
AGREE contracts must be syntactically valid and refer to existing AADL features or declared helpers.
Minimize repairs to only fix the reported diagnostics without altering unrelated architecture.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component type scopes are for assumptions/guarantees; implementation scopes are for assignments.
Constants and equations in AGREE must use valid scalar types: int, real, bool.
anti_patterns: Adding new subcomponents or ports to fix AGREE type errors instead of resolving references.
Introducing package-qualified identifiers that are not declared in the AADL model.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about missing entity references
AGREE validator diagnostics about unresolved connection ends
